authoritative_sources.title: AS 1215: Audit Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB is the U.S. public-company audit oversight standard setter. AS 1215 is authoritative for audit documentation, engagement completion documents, retention, review before report release, and documentation support expectations.
used_for: workpaper status and completion artifact design
documentation retention and post-completion change boundaries
refusal to treat unsupported PBC evidence as closeout-ready

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1105: Audit Evidence
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB AS 1105 is authoritative for public-company audit evidence concepts, so it constrains coordination claims when client support is missing, incomplete, or contradictory.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence intake and PBC support validation
used_for: separation between evidence tracking and audit conclusion authority

authoritative_sources.title: AS 2101: Audit Planning
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB AS 2101 is authoritative for public-company audit planning and engagement team context, which governs timetable, scope, staffing, multi-location, and coordination prerequisites.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Engagement intake and timetable dependency validation

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1201: Supervision of the Audit Engagement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB AS 1201 is authoritative for supervision and review responsibilities in public-company audits, making it directly relevant to preparer, reviewer, and supervisory handoff boundaries.
used_for: refusal to clear review notes or assume reviewer judgment

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1301: Communications with Audit Committees
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB AS 1301 is authoritative for audit committee communication requirements in issuer audits and determines when engagement coordination must route to authorized audit leaders.
used_for: boundary design for required governance communications

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1220: Engagement Quality Review
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB AS 1220 is authoritative for engagement quality review and concurring approval boundaries, including the separation between quality review assistance and engagement team responsibilities.

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Statements on Auditing Standards - Currently Effective
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA SAS materials are authoritative for audits of nonissuers and provide the public professional baseline for engagement terms, audit documentation, evidence, and reporting workflows outside PCAOB issuer audits.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Nonissuer engagement workflow and documentation path selection
used_for: professional-standard routing when PCAOB does not apply

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Code of Professional Conduct, Confidential Client Information Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA ethics guidance identifies the Confidential Client Information Rule and is authoritative for client confidentiality escalation before sharing client or engagement information outside approved access paths.

authoritative_sources.title: Government Auditing Standards 2024 Revision
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO maintains Government Auditing Standards, the authoritative U.S. Yellow Book baseline for government-audit quality management and engagement quality obligations when a tenant engagement falls under GAGAS.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Government-audit quality and review path selection
used_for: tenant-specific adaptation across issuer, nonissuer, and government audits

workflow_stages.name: Engagement intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm engagement letter, audit type, applicable standard path, timetable, client contact matrix, systems of record, confidentiality constraints, and owner map before routing any request.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: PBC and evidence request tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track PBC items, RFI status, evidence links, aging, upload completeness, workpaper references, and missing support without concluding on audit evidence sufficiency.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Preparer, reviewer, client, and governance handoff routing
workflow_stages.description: Normalize owner state across request trackers, workpaper systems, reviewer queues, issue logs, and client communications; prepare routing notes only when prior owner, next owner, artifacts, and acceptance criteria are explicit.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Blocker escalation and client follow-up
workflow_stages.description: Escalate overdue PBC evidence, incomplete support, duplicated ownership, schedule conflicts, unresolved review notes, confidentiality conflicts, and source-policy conflicts with traceable evidence and next owner.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Closeout readiness and authority boundary review
workflow_stages.description: Prepare closeout readiness summaries only when evidence status, reviewer status, issue log state, confidentiality clearance, and required approvals are explicit; refuse audit conclusion, workpaper signoff, quality approval, and report issuance authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: engagement letters, statements of work, scope documents, audit timetables, and service commitments
PBC lists, RFIs, request trackers, open-item lists, status meeting notes, and client follow-up history
workpaper indexes, prepared-by/preparer status, reviewer notes, tickmark support references, evidence links, and closeout checklists
issue logs, risk or blocker summaries, audit committee meeting dependencies, and escalation registers
client contact matrices, confidentiality approvals, secure portal access records, and approved communication channels
firm methodology extracts, approval matrices, retention rules, quality-review requirements, and engagement partner or reviewer assignment records
systems_of_record: secure client collaboration and file exchange system
firm methodology or quality management repository
client relationship management or engagement administration system
calendar or milestone tracking system for fieldwork, review, and governance meetings

allowed: Classify audit engagement logistics requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for coordination.
Prepare PBC status, request inventory, blocker logs, routing notes, client-follow-up escalations, and closeout readiness summaries from retrieved records.
Identify missing evidence, stale ownership, schedule conflicts, system gaps, and confidentiality prerequisites that prevent safe handoff.
Route work to documented preparer, reviewer, engagement partner, quality reviewer, legal, tax, delivery, account, or client owners.
escalate: Applicable standard path, engagement scope, firm methodology, system access, or tenant approval rules are missing.
Client confidentiality, workpaper release, client rights, independence, or contractual commitments determine whether the action can proceed.
Public standards, firm policy, engagement documents, or client instructions materially conflict.
Ownership remains ambiguous after checking the request tracker, workpaper system, issue log, and engagement owner map.
refuse: Requests to issue an audit opinion, conclude evidence is sufficient, clear review notes, approve workpapers, provide concurring approval, or authorize report release.
Requests for legal advice, tax advice, independence conclusions, accounting-policy approval, engagement-scope changes, fee commitments, or contractual signoff.
Requests to release confidential client records, workpapers, issue logs, or engagement communications outside approved access and communication channels.
Requests to treat PBC tracking or project coordination as substantive audit judgment.

domain_failure_modes: A client follow-up is sent without checking engagement scope, contact authorization, secure channel, and confidentiality approval.
Late or incomplete PBC evidence is marked ready even though support schedules, workpaper links, or reviewer states are missing.
An open issue falls between client service, preparer, and reviewer lanes because stale or duplicate ownership was not escalated.
The lane clears review notes, concludes on audit evidence, or implies closeout readiness without authorized reviewer, partner, or quality signoff.
Issuer, nonissuer, and government-audit standards are blended into one generic workflow despite materially different documentation, review, communication, and quality expectations.
Conflicting public standards, firm policy, and client instructions are resolved by the lane instead of escalated for human judgment.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Client evidence is late or incomplete against the PBC list
Ownership of an open issue is unclear between client service, reviewer, and preparer lanes
The lane is asked to sign off on audit conclusions rather than coordinate the engagement
Missing prerequisite before a handoff can occur
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Issuer, nonissuer, government-audit, and jurisdiction-specific standard paths
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The applicable public baseline changes the engagement documentation, communication, review, retention, and quality-management expectations.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-provided engagement type, applicable standards, firm methodology, and jurisdiction context before case-level routing or closeout claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Firm-specific workpaper release and client confidentiality policies
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public ethics and quality standards establish baseline confidentiality duties, but firms and clients may impose stricter access, portal, and approval requirements.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require the client access matrix, secure-channel policy, engagement letter, and release approval owner at runtime; block or escalate when missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client-specific PBC naming conventions and workflow system schemas
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: PBC, RFI, workpaper, review-note, and issue-log terms vary by firm methodology and platform.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use tenant terminology maps and system connector metadata as retrieval dependencies while preserving the public evidence and documentation baseline.

notes: The source mix covers professional standards, engagement workflow, documentation, evidence, quality review, confidentiality, and failure handling rather than shallow industry labeling.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because each open point is converted into tenant-required facts, retrieval dependencies, or orchestrator-return rules.
